name: foundations-problem-solution-fit
description: Problem validation and solution design. Use when discovering customer problems, generating solution hypotheses, or defining MVP scope.
capabilities: Discovers and validates customer problems using Jobs-to-be-Done framework
Generates and evaluates multiple solution hypotheses with effort-impact analysis
Analyzes existing alternatives to identify competitive advantage opportunities
Defines MVP scope with clear success metrics and development priorities
Identifies unique insights and defensible advantages for 10x improvement
author: BellaBe
securitySummary: Pure documentation skill containing only structured guidance and frameworks. Contains no executable code, no filesystem access, no network calls, and no external command execution. All static findings are false positives triggered by semantic misinterpretation of documentation text.
valueStatement: Building products without validating real customer problems leads to wasted investment. This skill provides structured frameworks to discover, prioritize, and validate problems before committing to solutions. It ensures strong problem-solution fit before significant development effort.
limitations: Does not conduct actual user interviews or gather primary research data
Does not build or prototype solutions - only designs and validates concepts
Does not access external market data or competitor information
Requires user-provided inputs like market intelligence and problem hypotheses

useCases.title: Validate startup ideas
useCases.description: Discover if your startup idea solves real customer problems worth paying for before building

useCases.title: Define MVP scope
useCases.description: Scope minimum viable product features that solve validated customer problems without overbuilding

useCases.title: Find 10x opportunities
useCases.description: Identify unique insights and defensible advantages to create breakthrough products

promptTemplates.title: Validate a problem
promptTemplates.scenario: Check if a problem is worth solving
promptTemplates.prompt: Help me validate that [specific problem] is a real problem worth solving for [target customer segment]. Use the Problem Discovery workflow to identify the problem, measure its frequency and intensity, and provide evidence from user interviews or market data.

promptTemplates.title: Generate solutions
promptTemplates.prompt: Generate 5 different solution approaches for solving [validated problem]. Evaluate each for technical feasibility, effort versus impact, and recommend the best option with build versus buy analysis.

promptTemplates.title: Analyze alternatives
promptTemplates.prompt: Analyze existing solutions to [customer problem] including direct competitors, indirect competitors, and manual workarounds. Identify switching barriers and unmet needs that create competitive advantage opportunities.

promptTemplates.title: Define MVP
promptTemplates.prompt: Define an MVP for [solution concept]. List core features that directly solve the validated problem, estimate development effort for each, define success metrics for launch, and identify pivot triggers if metrics are not met.

bestPractices: Follow the pain by focusing on high-frequency, high-intensity problems with clear evidence
Diverge then converge by generating multiple solution options before selecting one
Prototype cheaply by testing concepts with mockups before committing to build
antiPatterns: Asking Would you use X creates false positives; observe actual behavior instead
Solving problems you have rather than problems your customers actually face
Pursuing incremental improvements that cannot overcome switching costs in crowded markets

faq.question: Which AI tools support this skill?
faq.answer: This skill works with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code. It provides structured prompting frameworks.

faq.question: What inputs does this skill require?
faq.answer: Required inputs are market intelligence output and validated problem hypotheses from your research.

faq.question: How does this integrate with other skills?
faq.answer: Receives market context from market-intelligence and provides validated problems to other skills.

faq.question: Is my data safe with this skill?
faq.answer: This is a prompt-based skill with no code execution. All processing happens within your AI tool.

faq.question: Why is my output empty or incomplete?
faq.answer: Provide sufficient input context like market segments and problem hypotheses for best results.

faq.question: How is this different from market-intelligence?
faq.answer: Market-intelligence focuses on markets and competitors. This skill focuses on validating customer problems.
